message:
test(sse): add comprehensive proptest coverage for SSE parser robustness

Expand the SSE parser test suite with property-based tests covering edge
cases that unit tests alone would miss:

**New test helpers**: Add SseStream-based chunk parsing utilities
(split_bytes, parse_stream_chunks, parse_stream_single_chunk,
parse_stream_chunked, parse_stream_chunked_limited) that exercise the
async streaming path alongside the synchronous SseParser, ensuring both
produce identical results.

**Line ending invariant**: Verify that CR, LF, and CRLF line endings
produce identical parse results regardless of chunk boundaries, testing
the spec requirement that all three line endings are equivalent.

**ID field null-byte rejection**: Fuzz the id field with strings
containing embedded NUL bytes, verifying the spec rule that ids with
null bytes are silently ignored.

**Retry field fuzzing**: Generate arbitrary retry values (valid u64,
overflow, negative, alphabetic, empty) and verify last-assignment-wins
semantics with only valid u64 values being accepted.

**Duplicate field semantics**: Test spec-compliant behavior when multiple
event/id/data fields appear in a single event block (last event wins,
last valid id wins, data lines concatenated with newlines).

**Oversized data round-trip**: Test multi-megabyte data payloads (1KB to
3MB) parse and reconstruct correctly, verifying no truncation or
corruption in large payloads.

**Stream chunking invariant**: Verify that SseStream produces identical
events regardless of how the input bytes are chunked (single-byte to
multi-KB chunks), exercising the internal buffering logic.

Increase base proptest case count from 64 to 256 for better coverage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
